title: pimpinella dendroselinum
intro: Pimpinella dendroselinum, also known as Selinum palustre or Marsh Chervil, is a perennial plant native to Europe and Asia, belonging to the carrot family (Apiaceae). The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to reduce inflammation, ease digestive issues, and act as a diuretic. The health benefits of Pimpinella dendroselinum include its potential to lower blood pressure, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues such as bronchitis. The plant is typically prepared as an infusion or tincture for its herbal remedies, with the flowers and leaves being used for medicinal purposes.
